articles.title: 'From Darkness' crime drama filmed in Rochdale now available to stream for free
articles.excerpt: The BBC crime drama 'From Darkness', filmed in Rochdale, is now available to watch for free on U, offering viewers a gripping storyline and strong performances.
articles.content_html:
A crime drama called 'From Darkness', which was filmed in Rochdale, is now streaming for free on the platform U. The series originally aired on the BBC in 2015 and follows former police officer Claire Church, portrayed by Anne-Marie Duff, as she grapples with an unsolved serial killer case and her own troubled past.

The show features various locations, including Lower Falinge in Rochdale, and centres around Claire's return to Manchester after leaving due to the unsettling nature of a previous investigation. The narrative unfolds as she attempts to connect the dots between past and present cases linked to the serial killer.

In addition to Anne-Marie Duff, the cast includes Johnny Harris, Caroline Lee-Johnson, and Richard Rankin, among others. The series has received positive reviews for its engaging storyline and performances, with viewers praising its ability to keep them on the edge of their seats.

'From Darkness' is now available for streaming, allowing fans of crime dramas to enjoy the series at no cost. The production has been noted for its compelling narrative and strong character development, making it a worthwhile watch for those interested in the genre. The filming locations in Rochdale add a local context that may appeal to viewers familiar with the area.

articles.title: Pakistan blames Britain for Rochdale rapist’s crimes
articles.excerpt: Pakistan has issued a statement blaming Britain for the crimes committed by a rapist linked to Rochdale, raising questions about accountability and justice.
articles.content_html:
Pakistan has issued a statement blaming Britain for the crimes committed by a rapist linked to Rochdale. The comments come amid ongoing discussions about the responsibilities of authorities in addressing such serious offences.

The statement from Pakistani officials highlights concerns regarding the handling of the case and the implications for international relations. It suggests that Britain should take greater accountability for the actions of individuals who commit crimes abroad.

This development has sparked debate about the effectiveness of cross-border cooperation in tackling crime and ensuring justice for victims.

As the situation unfolds, it remains to be seen how this will affect diplomatic relations between the two nations.

articles.title: Edenfield Road to Close for Roadworks from 15 August 2026
articles.excerpt: Edenfield Road in Rochdale will be temporarily closed for roadworks starting on 15 August 2026, affecting traffic flow in the area.
articles.content_html:
Part of Edenfield Road will be temporarily closed from 15 August 2026. The closure will begin 10 metres west of the junction with Overtown Lane and extend 674 metres in an easterly direction.

The roadworks are expected to impact traffic flow on Edenfield Road and surrounding routes, including Spotland Road, Sandy Lane, and Bury Road.

Drivers may need to seek alternative routes during this period. Further details about the roadworks and affected areas can be found on the Rochdale Borough Council website.

Residents and commuters are advised to plan their journeys accordingly as the road closure may lead to delays.

articles.title: Lost iPhone at Rochdale Tram Stop: Appeal for Information
articles.excerpt: A woman has appealed for help after losing her daughter's iPhone at a tram stop in Rochdale. The phone, in a white polka dot case, was misplaced around 3:40 PM today.
articles.content_html:
A woman has appealed for help after losing her daughter's iPhone at a tram stop in Rochdale. The phone, which is white and has a polka dot case, was misplaced around 3:40 PM today.

The woman stated that the phone was a birthday present for her daughter, who received it just yesterday. She is hopeful that someone may have found it and can return it.

Anyone who has information regarding the lost phone is encouraged to come forward. The woman expressed her gratitude in advance for any assistance.

The incident occurred at the tram stop facing the bus depot, and the woman is eager to recover the phone as it holds sentimental value.

The decision was made during a recent council meeting, where members expressed confidence in Murphy's ability to lead the organisation. Murphy brings a wealth of experience in local government and is expected to focus on improving services and engaging with the
articles.content_html:
ONGOING STORY — Updated 17 July 2026 at 14:34. 2 source updates have been combined into this article.

Rochdale has appointed Murphy as its new chief executive. The decision was made during a recent council meeting, where members expressed confidence in Murphy's ability to lead the organisation. Murphy brings a wealth of experience in local government and is expected to focus on improving services and engaging with the community.

Latest update

Rochdale has appointed Murphy as its new chief executive. The decision was made during a recent council meeting, where members expressed confidence in Murphy's ability to lead the organisation.

Murphy brings a wealth of experience in local government and is expected to focus on improving services and engaging with the community.

The appointment follows a thorough selection process, with Murphy emerging as the preferred candidate due to their track record in similar roles.

Council officials are optimistic that Murphy's leadership will help drive forward key initiatives and address the challenges facing the borough.

Earlier developments

Rochdale Borough Council has confirmed Julie Murphy as its new Chief Executive. The appointment received approval during a meeting of the full council.

Julie Murphy will take on the role following the council's decision, which was made public on 17 July 2026.

The new Chief Executive is expected to lead the council in its ongoing efforts to serve the community and manage local services effectively.

Further details regarding her plans and initiatives for the council are anticipated in the coming weeks.

articles.title: University of Manchester Student Researches Garden Management in Rochdale
articles.excerpt: A master's student at the University of Manchester is conducting research on garden management preferences in Rochdale, focusing on biodiversity targets.
articles.content_html:
A master's student at the University of Manchester is currently completing her dissertation on garden management preferences in Rochdale, UK. The research aims to understand how these preferences impact local and national biodiversity targets.

The student, called Natalie, is exploring various aspects of garden management and its implications for the environment. Her work seeks to contribute to the broader conversation about biodiversity and sustainable practices in urban areas.

Natalie's research is particularly relevant to Rochdale Borough residents, as it addresses local gardening habits and their potential effects on biodiversity. The findings may inform future initiatives aimed at enhancing ecological awareness and practices within the community.

The study highlights the importance of individual choices in gardening and their collective impact on the environment, aligning with national efforts to meet biodiversity goals.

articles.title: First-time buyer takes over Croft Shifa Pharmacy in Rochdale
articles.excerpt: Croft Shifa Pharmacy on Belfield Road has been sold to first-time buyer Hossiendad Hossieni, marking a new chapter for the health centre pharmacy.
articles.content_html:
Croft Shifa Pharmacy, located on Belfield Road, has been sold to a first-time buyer following a deal completed by specialist advisers. The pharmacy dispenses an average of 6,400 items each month and operates as a standard-hours health centre pharmacy.

The new owner, Hossiendad Hossieni of NHSR Limited, recognised the potential of the pharmacy, which has been run under full management for some time. Tom Young, senior business agent at Christie & Co, who handled the sale, noted that Greater Manchester remains a hub for pharmacy transactions, with high demand from various buyer types.

Mr Young expressed confidence in Mr Hossieni's ability to grow the business, stating that he sees great potential for improvement in the pharmacy's capture rate from the integrated health centre.

The sale represents a significant opportunity for Mr Hossieni as he embarks on his journey in pharmacy ownership, and Mr Young wished him the best for the future.

articles.title: Albion Inn raises £1,090 for Rochdale MS Society
articles.excerpt: The Albion Inn in Shawclough has raised £1,090 for the Rochdale branch of the MS Society following a community charity event.
articles.summary: Customers, organisers and supporters at the Albion Inn came together to raise £1,090 for MS Society Rochdale, with the pub thanking Natasha Jones and Craig Jones for coordinating the event and donations.
articles.content_html:
The Albion Inn in Shawclough has raised £1,090 for the Rochdale branch of the MS Society following a community charity event.

The pub announced the final total after donations and fundraising activities were brought together, thanking local residents and supporters for helping make the event a success.

Community effort raises four-figure total

The money was raised through a team effort involving customers, organisers and members of the wider community.

The Albion Inn gave special thanks to Natasha Jones and Craig Jones for organising the event programme and coordinating donations.

“Thank you once again to the whole community for your efforts. Great team effort by all.”

Supporting people affected by multiple sclerosis

The £1,090 donation will support MS Society Rochdale and its work with people affected by multiple sclerosis.

Multiple sclerosis is a condition affecting the brain and spinal cord and can cause a wide range of symptoms, including problems with movement, balance, vision and fatigue.

Local MS Society groups can provide information, social support and practical help for people living with the condition, as well as their families and carers.

Thanks to everyone who contributed

The pub thanked everyone who donated, attended or helped organise the fundraising effort.

The presentation image records the donation as £1,090 and is dated Saturday 11 July 2026.

This report is based on a public update and fundraising presentation image published by the Albion Inn following its charity event for MS Society Rochdale.

Members of Andys Man Club Rochdale attended the Falinge Park Classic Car and Bike Show, hosted by the Rotary Club of Rochdale. The event showcased a variety of classic cars and bikes, attracting enthusiasts from the local area.

During the show, members of Andys Man Club had the opportunity to meet the newly appointed Mayor Faisal Rana and his wife. The Mayor expressed support for the initiatives of Andys Man Club, which focuses on mental health awareness and support for men.

The presence of the Mayor at the event highlights the importance of community engagement and support for local organisations. Andys Man Club aims to provide a safe space for men to discuss their mental health and wellbeing.

The Falinge Park Classic Car and Bike Show provided a platform for local groups to connect and promote their causes, fostering a sense of community involvement.

A classic car show is taking place today in Falinge Park, organised by the Rotary Club of Rochdale. The event features a range of classic vehicles on display, attracting car enthusiasts and families alike.

Presenters at the show are engaging with visitors and exhibitors, creating a lively atmosphere. Attendees have the opportunity to admire a selection of classic cars throughout the day.

The event is expected to draw a significant number of visitors, contributing to local community engagement and enjoyment. It provides a platform for classic car owners to showcase their vehicles and share their passion with others.

articles.title: Rochdale Council to Tighten Controls on Houses of Multiple Occupation
articles.excerpt: Rochdale Council is set to introduce tighter controls on houses of multiple occupation (HMOs) following recent protests in Heywood organised by Jamie Thomason
articles.content_html:
Rochdale Council is set to crack down on houses of multiple occupation (HMOs) in the borough. Reform councillors have called on town hall officials to implement stricter regulations. This decision follows protests against HMOs that took place over the weekend in Heywood.

Councillor Paul O’Neill stated that the council aims to introduce an Article 4 Direction to alleviate tensions surrounding HMOs. The issue was raised by Reform councillors during the latest town hall meeting. Article 4 directives would require all HMOs, regardless of size, to obtain specific planning permission. Currently, only HMOs accommodating seven or more people require such permission.

Councillor Lee Wolf from Reform expressed that while HMO licensing is important, it does not address the location of these properties or prevent excessive clustering. He noted that residents desire a greater say in the matter and believe that HMOs are impacting local services, particularly in specific areas.

The controlling Labour group indicated that it is already planning changes. Housing boss Councillor Danny Meredith thanked Reform for their support of the Labour plan. Fellow Labour councillor Richard Jackson added that they are actively working on the issue but must follow proper processes. He emphasised that implementing Article 4 requires substantial evidence and cannot be enacted immediately. The proposal is currently undergoing local authority processes and is expected to be presented to the full council for a formal vote later this year.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article was compiled from identified public sources and may be updated when further verified information becomes available.

articles.title: Man found guilty of murdering teenager over stolen e-bike in Middleton
articles.excerpt: Terrence King, 60, has been found guilty of murdering 15-year-old Devon Simmonds-Caines, who stole an e-bike from another boy in Middleton last year.
articles.content_html:
A man has been found guilty of murdering a teenager who stole an e-bike. Terrence King, 60, killed 15-year-old Devon Simmonds-Caines by deliberately driving his car into him in Middleton, Greater Manchester, on 22 July 2025.

The jury heard that Simmonds-Caines had stolen the £3,000 bike from another boy on the Langley Estate. Following a fracas, he rode off on the e-bike, prompting King to pursue him in his vehicle.

King admitted to manslaughter but denied murder. However, after a two-week trial, he was convicted of murder and is scheduled to be sentenced on 23 July at Minshull Street Crown Court in Manchester.

The courtroom erupted with screams of joy from the victim's family and friends as the verdict was announced, with shouts of "thank you" heard from those present. CCTV footage captured the moments leading up to the incident, showing the events that unfolded before Simmonds-Caines was struck.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article was compiled from identified public sources and may be updated when further verified information becomes available.

articles.title: Rochdale Councillors Approve Pay Rise Despite Cost of Living Concerns
articles.excerpt: Councillors in Rochdale have voted to increase their annual allowances despite ongoing cost of living challenges faced by residents.
articles.content_html:
A pay rise for Rochdale councillors has been approved following a vote at a town hall meeting on July 16. The current annual allowance for each elected councillor is £12,996.09, which will increase to £13,515 after the decision.

The increase, which covers basic costs for councillors to travel to meetings and conduct constituency work, sparked significant debate in the council chamber. Members of the Reform and Workers Party were particularly vocal in their opposition, with Coun Mohammed Shafiq stating that the pay rise was 'inappropriate' given the financial struggles many families in the borough are facing.

Leader of Reform, Coun John Taylor, echoed these sentiments, highlighting the ongoing cost of living crisis and questioning the appropriateness of the vote. In response, Labour councillors defended the increase, arguing that it remains lower than allowances in other Greater Manchester councils. Coun Kath Bromfield noted that the rise could help make politics more accessible to a wider range of people.

The council leader, Neil Emmott, pointed out that those who opposed the increase were not obligated to claim the full amount. The vote was passed by the controlling Labour group, and only the basic allowance was adjusted, with all other special allowances remaining unchanged.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article was compiled from identified public sources and may be updated when further verified information becomes available.

articles.title: Middleton McDonald's Reopens Following Major Redesign
articles.excerpt: McDonald's in Middleton has reopened after a significant refurbishment aimed at enhancing customer and staff experiences, featuring updated technology and improved facilities.
articles.content_html:
A McDonald's restaurant in Middleton has reopened following a major refurbishment designed to enhance the experience for both customers and staff. The redesign includes a new layout, updated technology, and separate facilities for delivery couriers.

The restaurant, owned by local franchisee Gerald Thompson, has undergone changes as part of McDonald's latest reimagining programme. This initiative aims to improve speed, efficiency, and choice for customers. The refurbishment features a redesigned kitchen and dining area that supports digital ordering through the McDonald's app, allowing customers to order in advance and either collect their food or have it delivered to their table upon arrival.

The front counter has been restructured to create dedicated areas for different ordering methods, which McDonald's states will reduce congestion and shorten queues around self-service ordering screens. Additionally, improvements have been made behind the scenes, including a larger order assembly area and dedicated preparation spaces for dine-in meals.

Gerald Thompson, who operates 18 McDonald's restaurants across Greater Manchester, expressed pride in reinvesting in the Middleton location, which he first opened as a franchisee in 2000. He noted that the redesign provides a fresh look and enhances the experience for both customers and the restaurant's team of over eighty staff members.

articles.title: Rochdale MP Paul Waugh Calls for Urgent Climate Action in Parliament
articles.excerpt: Local MP Paul Waugh has reiterated his call for immediate climate action, urging the government to enhance its efforts to protect the environment.
articles.content_html:
A local MP has called for urgent climate action in Parliament. Paul Waugh emphasised the need for the government to intensify its efforts to address environmental issues.

Waugh's statement comes amid growing concerns about climate change and its impact on communities. He challenged the government to take more decisive measures to protect the environment.

The MP's remarks highlight the importance of immediate action in tackling climate-related challenges. He has been vocal about the need for sustainable policies that benefit both the planet and local residents.

This call for action reflects a broader movement among politicians advocating for environmental responsibility and accountability.

articles.excerpt: Mark Dunning has announced his 21-man squad for the upcoming match against Rochdale Hornets, featuring one change from the previous week.
articles.content_html:
Men's Head Coach Mark Dunning has named his EnjoyTravel 21-man squad to face Rochdale Hornets this Sunday. The announcement comes ahead of the match scheduled for July 19, 2026.

There is one change from last week's squad, with Cian Tyrer replacing Oliver Roberts. The team will be looking to build on their recent performances as they prepare for the fixture.

articles.title: Rochdale Pub Named Among Best in the UK by The Telegraph
articles.excerpt: The Baum in Rochdale has been recognised in The Telegraph's annual list of the best pubs in the UK for 2026, praised for its food and welcoming atmosphere.
articles.content_html:
A pub in Rochdale has been named among the best in the UK. The Baum, located on Toad Lane, features in The Telegraph's annual list of The Best Pubs in the UK for 2026, which includes 500 pubs from England, 75 from Scotland, 50 from Wales, and 25 from Northern Ireland.

The selection process involved a panel of judges who assessed each pub based on criteria such as character, staff hospitality, and historical significance. Nineteen pubs from Greater Manchester were recognised, with The Baum standing out for its food offerings, including traditional Bury black pudding served in a scotch egg and on the breakfast menu.

Despite being converted from a hardware shop in the 1980s, The Baum has garnered a reputation for its inviting atmosphere and diverse menu. Patrons can enjoy a variety of pub classics, including fish and chips and pies, as well as smaller plates like seafood tacos and lamb kofta tacos.

The recognition highlights the pub's appeal to locals and visitors alike, particularly those travelling from nearby areas such as Bury. The inclusion in this prestigious list underscores the quality and charm of The Baum as a dining destination.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article was compiled from identified public sources and may be updated when further verified information becomes available.

articles.title: Missing Cat Gene Last Seen in Falinge Area of Rochdale
articles.excerpt: A grey and white cat named Gene has gone missing from the Falinge area of Rochdale. He is microchipped but not wearing a collar.
articles.content_html:
A cat named Gene has been reported missing from the Falinge area of Rochdale. The grey and white cat was last seen a couple of days ago.

Gene is microchipped but is not wearing a collar. He is known to be an outdoor cat, often coming and going as he pleases.

His owner has expressed concern, noting that it is unusual for him not to return home. Anyone who may have seen Gene or has information about his whereabouts is encouraged to reach out.

The missing cat's last known location was near Wasilla Drive and Clark Street in Rochdale. Residents in the area are asked to keep an eye out for him.

articles.title: Community Payback Workers Maintain Whitworth Cemetery Grounds
articles.excerpt: A team of community payback workers has been seen mowing and strimming around graves at Whitworth Road Cemetery, demonstrating respect for the site while maintaining its appearance.
articles.content_html:
A team of community payback workers has been observed mowing and strimming around graves at Whitworth Road Cemetery. The workers were noted for their respectful approach to the headstones as they carried out their tasks.

The maintenance work was witnessed by a visitor who had come to clean and strim two graves. The visitor expressed appreciation for the efforts of the community payback team, highlighting their dedication to the upkeep of the cemetery.

Community payback schemes are designed to provide offenders with the opportunity to give back to the community through unpaid work. This initiative not only helps maintain local spaces but also fosters a sense of responsibility among participants.

Whitworth Road Cemetery serves as a significant site for the local community, and the ongoing maintenance efforts contribute to its upkeep and respect for those interred there.

articles.excerpt: A councillor attended the Full Council meeting at Rochdale Town Hall, advocating for the residents of North Heywood and supporting an amendment to an opposition motion.
articles.content_html:

During the meeting, the councillor supported an amendment to an opposition motion proposed by the Labour and Co-operative controlling group. This amendment aimed to address specific concerns raised by the community.

The Full Council meeting provided a platform for various issues affecting the ward and the town to be discussed, highlighting the councillor's commitment to local governance.

articles.title: Traffic Disruption in Heywood Due to Middleton Road Changes
articles.excerpt: Traffic in Heywood has been severely disrupted today, partly due to the ongoing pedestrianisation of Middleton Road, affecting routes throughout the town.
articles.content_html:
Traffic in Heywood has been severely disrupted today, partly due to the ongoing pedestrianisation of Middleton Road. Reports indicate that the changes are causing significant congestion across various routes in the town.

The alterations to Middleton Road are part of an experimental scheme aimed at improving pedestrian access. However, some residents have questioned the timing of the changes, particularly as other roadworks are also taking place in the area.

Witnesses described the situation as chaotic, with delays affecting not only the immediate vicinity of the 'Hopwood Triangle' but also extending throughout Heywood. The combination of roadworks and the new pedestrianisation measures has led to frustration among drivers and pedestrians alike.

Authorities have not yet commented on the situation or provided information regarding potential adjustments to the traffic management plan.

articles.title: Officers Conduct Off-Road Bike Operation in Rochdale
articles.excerpt: Officers from Rochdale North Neighbourhood, supported by various agencies, carried out an operation targeting anti-social behaviour involving off-road bikes.
articles.content_html:
Officers and PCSOs from Rochdale North Neighbourhood recently conducted a dedicated operation to address anti-social behaviour related to off-road bikes.

The operation was supported by colleagues from Rochdale East, the Special Constabulary, community volunteers, and United Utilities.

This initiative aimed to tackle issues arising from the use of off-road bikes in the area, which have been a concern for local residents.

Authorities continue to monitor the situation and encourage community involvement in reporting any further incidents.

articles.title: Rochdale Football Club Celebrates Promotion at Classic Car Show
articles.excerpt: Rochdale Football Club will celebrate its promotion back to the football league at the Classic Car Show this Sunday, following a dramatic playoff final victory at Wembley in May.
articles.content_html:
Rochdale Football Club will celebrate its promotion back to the football league at the Classic Car Show this Sunday. The club secured its place in the league in a dramatic playoff final at Wembley in May.

The event will provide an opportunity for fans and the community to come together and commemorate the team's achievement. Details about the show and its activities have been shared on social media.

This celebration follows a successful season for the club, which saw them return to the football league after a period of absence. The playoff final victory has been described as one of the most thrilling moments in the club's recent history.

The Classic Car Show is expected to attract a large crowd, with various activities planned to mark the occasion. Fans are encouraged to attend and join in the festivities.
